perturbation.original_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it shall not make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except for LOA purposes; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only,to those of its employees who need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it shall not copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party, except to the extent necessary for the LOA purposes; and e) that it shall keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 2 Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it may make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party for purposes other than LOA purposes with the Disclosing Party’s written consent; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only to its employees, regardless of whether they need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it may copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party; and e) that it is not required to keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing Clause 2, specifically subsections b), c), d), and e), this creates in-text contradictions with the original intent of the NDA. Originally, the Receiving Party was strictly limited to using the Confidential Information only for LOA purposes, but now they can use it for other purposes with written consent, contradicting the original restriction. Similarly, access was limited to employees needing it for LOA purposes, but now all employees can have access. Reproduction of information was previously limited, now it has been expanded. Lastly, the requirement to keep record of information was cancelled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the terms of the NDA the Receiving Party hereby undertakes to the Disclosing Party: a) that it shall treat the Confidential Information directly or indirectly disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party as strictly confidential and will not without obtaining the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party disclose any part of the Confidential Information to any third party, except where compelled by law or regulation; b) that it may make use of any part of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party for purposes other than LOA purposes with the Disclosing Party’s written consent; c) that it shall give access to the Confidential Information only to its employees, regardless of whether they need access to the Confidential Information for LOA purposes and will ensure that such employees adhere to the obligations and restrictions contained in the NDA; d) that it may copy or reproduce in any form any of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the Disclosing Party; and e) that it is not required to keep a current record of all copies and reproductions of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Clause 2 introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text limited the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information strictly to LOA purposes, access to employees needing it for LOA purposes, limited reproduction of the information, and required keeping records. However, the changed text allows the Receiving Party to use it for other purposes with consent, gives all employees access, allows reproduction, and removes the record-keeping requirement. This directly conflicts with the original NDA's intent, as explained in the perturbation explanation and further evidenced by the fact that the contradicted text is the same as the changed text indicating the changes directly impact the initial agreement, leading to an incompatibility and ambiguity about the obligations and rights of the parties.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 9 Neither Party may assign or transfer (including but not limited to sublicense) any of its rights or obligations under the NDA.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 9 Either Party may assign or transfer (including but not limited to sublicense) any of its rights or obligations under the NDA with written approval.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that neither party can transfer any of their rights, with the new clause making it the opposite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Either Party may assign or transfer (including but not limited to sublicense) any of its rights or obligations under the NDA with written approval.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited any assignment or transfer of rights. The changed text now permits assignment or transfer with written approval. The contradicted text mirrors the changed text, allowing assignment or transfer with written approval. This directly contradicts the original restriction, creating a clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Clause 3 The obligations under the NDA do not apply to information of the Disclosing Party which is: a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter available to the public generally through no failure on the part of the Receiving Party; b) known to the Receiving Party at the time of its disclosure; c) disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party having the right to disclose Confidential Information and without restrictions as to use or disclosure; or d) developed independently by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information received.
perturbation.changed_text: Clause 3 The obligations under the NDA apply to all information of the Disclosing Party, even if: a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter available to the public generally through no failure on the part of the Receiving Party; b) known to the Receiving Party at the time of its disclosure; c) disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party having the right to disclose Confidential Information and without restrictions as to use or disclosure; or d) developed independently by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information received.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the obligations apply to all information regardless of the conditions in the bullets, it is directly contradicting the original clause that said information is not applied to the bullets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations under the NDA apply to all information of the Disclosing Party, even if: a) at the time of disclosure or thereafter available to the public generally through no failure on the part of the Receiving Party; b) known to the Receiving Party at the time of its disclosure; c) disclosed to the Receiving Party by a third party having the right to disclose Confidential Information and without restrictions as to use or disclosure; or d) developed independently by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information received.
perturbation.justification: The original text (Clause 3) states that the obligations under the NDA do *not* apply to certain types of information. The changed text states that the obligations *do* apply to all information, even the types listed in the bullets. The contradicted text restates the changed text, meaning there is a clear and direct conflict between the original text and the subsequent contradicted text, as the obligations are both stated to not apply and to apply to the same information. This fulfills the definition of a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction. The explanation provided also supports that the change causes a contradiction.
